senses.definition: a sport in which two or four players use rackets to hit a small ball backward and forward over a net

examples.x: She won the tennis tournament and received a trophy.
They play tennis as a way to stay active and fit.

senses.definition: a sport played by two or four players who hit a lightweight object called a shuttle back and forth over a tall net using rackets

examples.x: He used a powerful smash in badminton to win the point.
They played badminton in the park on a sunny afternoon.

senses.definition: a game played on a table by two or four players who bounce a small ball on the table over a net using special rackets

examples.x: During our office break, we often play table tennis.
He takes table tennis lessons every weekend.
My sister is the best table tennis player in our family.
She is practicing hard to compete in the national table tennis tournament.
Table tennis is a great way to spend time with friends.

senses.definition: a game that involves two or more players, hitting a rubber ball against the walls of a closed court by a racket

examples.x: The objective of squash is to hit the ball against the front wall in a way that makes it difficult for the opponent to return.

senses.definition: a historical indoor racket sport played in an enclosed court with various walls and openings, using a complex set of rules and scoring

examples.x: Learning the rules of real tennis can be challenging.
The court for real tennis is different from regular tennis courts.
Real tennis is often considered the predecessor to modern tennis.
He enjoys playing real tennis at the historic court.
He studied the history of real tennis for his research project.

senses.definition: a game for two or four people who play with a hollow rubber ball and special rackets on a court with four walls
to hit the ball in a way that makes it spin and curve sideways or at an angle, often making it bounce low and in a different direction

examples.x: She sliced the ball perfectly, making it curve away from her opponent's reach.
The player often slices her backhand to keep opponents off balance.
He sliced the ball with precision, keeping it close to the net.
Their coach taught them how to slice effectively during practice.
He prefers to slice his serves to disrupt his opponent's rhythm.

senses.definition: a powerful shot with great force and speed, often seen in games like tennis or badminton

examples.x: The tennis player won the point with a powerful smash down the line.
His badminton smash caught his opponent off guard, scoring the winning point.
The volleyball player's smash was too strong for the opposing team to defend.
She executed a perfect overhead smash in the squash match.
The badminton player practiced his smash technique to improve his game.

senses.definition: a stroke in racket sports such as tennis or table tennis where the player hits the ball with forward spin

examples.x: She used topspin to keep the ball deep in the court.
A well-executed topspin can be difficult to counter.
He adjusted his grip to control the amount of topspin on his shots.
He added more wrist action to generate more topspin on his backhand.

senses.definition: the act of hitting the ball back to the opponent after it has been served or hit by the opponent in racket sports like tennis and table tennis

examples.x: He struggled to anticipate his opponent's return.
She practiced her returns to improve her consistency.
His return down the line caught his opponent off guard.
He focused on his footwork to improve his returns.
Her return was powerful and deep, putting pressure on her opponent.

senses.definition: a softly hit shot in sports like tennis or badminton, aimed to land just over the net and close to the opponent's side

examples.x: She surprised her opponent with a perfectly executed drop shot.
His drop shot just cleared the net, leaving his opponent stranded.
The tennis player's drop shot floated just out of reach of her opponent.
The badminton player won the point with a deceptive drop shot.
He used a drop shot to break his opponent's rhythm during the match.

senses.definition: a score of zero indicating that a player has not yet scored in a given set in sports like tennis and squash

examples.x: She secured a love game with four straight aces.
He served an ace to win the game at love.

senses.definition: (racket sports) a serve that the opponent fails to touch with their racket

examples.x: Her powerful serve produced several aces during the match.
The ace was so fast that her opponent didn't even react.
He celebrated after hitting his first ace of the match.
Aces are crucial for maintaining momentum in a game.
She aimed for the corners to increase her chances of scoring an ace.

senses.definition: a delicate stroke played close to the net to gently drop the shuttlecock over

examples.x: She executed a perfect net shot to catch her opponent off guard.
His net shot barely cleared the net, landing just inside the opponent's court.
Maria's deceptive net shot forced her opponent into a weak return.
The net shot requires precise control and soft touch to execute effectively.
He practiced his net shot tirelessly to master its finesse.

senses.definition: the way a player holds the racket to hit a backhand stroke

examples.x: She adjusted her backhand grip before hitting the shot.
His coach taught him a proper backhand grip for better control.
He practiced his backhand grip to improve his backhand strokes.
Her backhand grip was too tight, causing her shots to go wide.
Switching to a backhand grip, he returned the ball with ease.

senses.definition: a stroke in racket sports where the player hits the ball with the back of their dominant hand facing the direction of the shot

examples.x: She played a precise backhand shot down the line.
His backhand shot caught his opponent off guard.
The backhand shot requires good timing.
He used slice on his backhand shot to keep it low.
Her opponent attacked her backhand shot.

senses.definition: a way of holding the racket to hit the ball or shuttlecock with the front side of the hand facing forward

examples.x: She adjusted her forehand grip to add more spin to the shot.
He switched to a forehand grip before hitting the next shot.
His forehand grip helped him execute a perfect cross-court shot.
She practiced her forehand grip to enhance her accuracy.
The forehand grip felt more natural to him during the match.

senses.definition: a stroke in racket sports like squash where the player hits the ball on their dominant side with a forward swing motion

examples.x: She unleashed a powerful forehand drive down the line.
His forehand drive forced his opponent into defensive mode.
The forehand drive is a fundamental offensive shot.
She practiced her forehand drive to improve her consistency.
Her opponent struggled to handle her deep forehand drives.

senses.definition: a stroke in racket sports like tennis and squash where the player hits the ball on their dominant side with a forward swing motion

examples.x: His forehand shot caught his opponent off guard.
She played a precise forehand shot down the line.
Her opponent attacked her forehand shot with aggressive returns.
She struggled with her forehand shot during the match.
He improved his forehand shot through regular practice.

senses.definition: a way of holding the paddle in table tennis similar to shaking hands, with the thumb and index finger around the handle

examples.x: Beginners often start with the shakehand grip due to its simplicity.
His shakehand grip gave him a strong advantage in rallies.
The shakehand grip is popular in Western countries for table tennis.
With the shakehand grip, he could generate more spin on his serves.
Her coach recommended the shakehand grip for a more balanced game.

senses.definition: a stroke in racket sports like badminton and tennis where the player hits the ball on their non-dominant side with a forward swing motion

examples.x: Her backhand drive forced her opponent into defensive positions.
The backhand drive requires good wrist and forearm strength.
She practiced her backhand drive to improve her consistency.
He executed a powerful backhand drive down the line.
Her opponent struggled to handle her deep backhand drives.

senses.definition: a powerful strike with the dominant hand's racket side in racket sports

examples.x: His opponent couldn't return his forehand smash.
She won the point with a perfectly executed forehand smash.
Rafael Nadal's forehand smash is one of the most feared shots in tennis.
The forehand smash is a key weapon in her arsenal during crucial points.
With perfect timing and technique, he delivered a winning forehand smash.

senses.definition: (racket sports) the rotational spin applied to the ball, causing it to curve or swerve sideways during flight

examples.x: Sidespin adds unpredictability.
His serve had sidespin, making it tricky.
The sidespin caused the ball to curve.
Applying sidespin changes the ball's path.
His backhand had subtle sidespin.

senses.definition: a type of spin applied to the ball in racket sports, where the ball rotates backwards as it moves through the air

examples.x: She used backspin to keep the ball low and close to the net.
His backspin shot dropped sharply after crossing the net.
He practiced adding backspin to his serves for better placement.
He adjusted his racket angle to increase the amount of backspin.
He used backspin to counter his opponent's aggressive play.

senses.definition: (table tennis) a regulation that speeds up play by requiring a point to be awarded if a rally lasts longer than a specified time limit

examples.x: The expedite rule came into effect after the rally extended beyond ten minutes.
Under the expedite rule, if a rally exceeds a set time limit, a point is awarded to the server.
Both players were cautious not to trigger the expedite rule during their intense rally.
The expedite rule adds an element of urgency to longer rallies.
Players need to anticipate the expedite rule when engaging in defensive play.

senses.definition: a fault that occurs when a player's foot touches or crosses the boundary line during a serve in a racket sport

examples.x: The umpire called a foot fault when his toe grazed the baseline during the serve.
She lost the point due to a foot fault, as her foot touched the baseline while serving.
The player was warned by the referee for committing multiple foot faults.
His frustration grew as he repeatedly committed foot faults during the match.
A foot fault can result in the loss of the serve in tennis.

senses.definition: a stroke in racket sports where the ball or shuttlecock is hit with the racket's frame instead of the strings

examples.x: She mishit the ball, resulting in a wood shot that barely crossed the net.
His opponent's wood shot gave him an easy point to win the game.
Despite her efforts, she couldn't avoid the occasional wood shot during the match.
The wood shot caused the shuttlecock to fly off in an unpredictable direction.
The player's lack of focus led to several wood shots in a row.

senses.definition: (racket sports) a serve that does not count because of a small problem, like the ball hitting the net or a distraction, and is played again

examples.x: The umpire called a let when the ball hit the net during the serve.
Her serve was a let because someone in the crowd moved.
The let was replayed due to a slight issue.
The point was replayed after a let was called.

senses.definition: a shot where the player hits the ball with power and speed in racket sports such as tennis and squash

examples.x: She unleashed a powerful drive down the line for a winner.
His drive forced his opponent into a defensive position.
The drive requires good technique and timing.
A well-executed drive can dictate the pace of the game.
The coach emphasized footwork for a more effective drive.

senses.definition: a topspin stroke executed with a backhand motion, typically in table tennis or badminton

examples.x: His opponent struggled to return his powerful backhand loop.
The backhand loop is a challenging stroke to master due to its timing and technique.
Maria's backhand loop has improved significantly since she started training.
He surprised his opponent with a sharp backhand loop down the line.
The backhand loop is a key technique for attacking high balls in table tennis.

senses.definition: a racket sport played on a smaller court surrounded by walls

examples.x: Paddle tennis is gaining popularity among younger athletes.
She practiced paddle tennis every Saturday morning.
The paddle tennis tournament will be held next month.
Paddle tennis requires agility and quick reflexes.
The rules of paddle tennis are similar to those of tennis.

senses.definition: a paddle sport that combines elements of tennis, badminton, and ping-pong, played on a smaller court

examples.x: She won the pickleball tournament with her powerful serves.
Pickleball is becoming increasingly popular among all age groups.
Have you tried pickleball yet?
He twisted his ankle playing pickleball and had to sit out for a week.
Pickleball etiquette includes calling out the score clearly after each point.

senses.definition: a racket sport played on a raised platform enclosed by wire fencing

examples.x: They practice platform tennis every Saturday morning.
Platform tennis is gaining popularity among winter sports enthusiasts.
She won the platform tennis championship last season.
The rules of platform tennis are similar to regular tennis but with some unique variations.
He sprained his ankle playing platform tennis last week.

senses.definition: a racket sport played on an enclosed court smaller than a tennis court

examples.x: She prefers padel over tennis because of its faster pace and shorter matches.
His favorite padel strategy involves using the walls to his advantage.
Padel tournaments attract players of all skill levels, from amateurs to professionals.

senses.definition: a sport similar to badminton that is played with specially designed rackets and a shuttlecock

examples.x: The rules of crossminton are easy to learn for beginners.
Our team won the crossminton tournament last month.
The shuttlecock used in crossminton is designed for higher wind resistance.

senses.definition: a racket sport, typically played on a court similar to squash or racquetball

examples.x: I’ve been practicing Qianball every day to improve my hand-eye coordination.
The Qianball court was buzzing with energy as the players warmed up for the match.
The unique rules of Qianball make it a challenging yet exciting sport to learn.
The Qianball tournament attracted players from all over the country, showcasing the sport’s growing popularity.
Training for Qianball has significantly improved my agility and reflexes.

senses.definition: a sport that combines table tennis, badminton, squash, and tennis into a single match

examples.x: She won the racketlon championship last year.
He trains daily to improve his racketlon performance.
He excelled in squash but struggled in the tennis segment of racketlon.
Her favorite part of racketlon is playing badminton.

senses.definition: a racket sport played with a rubber ball and rackets on a three-walled court, similar to squash and handball

examples.x: Frontenis combines elements of tennis and squash, making it fast-paced.
She excelled in frontenis, winning several local tournaments.
Frontenis requires quick reflexes and excellent hand-eye coordination.
He injured his wrist while playing an intense game of frontenis.

senses.definition: a fast-paced racket sport originating in the Basque region, played in a three-walled court using a curved basket to fling a ball against the front wall

examples.x: He trained for years to master the techniques of jai alai.
He attended a jai alai tournament featuring top players.
The players wear protective gear due to the speed of the ball in jai alai.

senses.definition: a Brazilian beach sport played with wooden rackets and a small rubber ball

examples.x: The frescobol match attracted a large crowd.
They practiced frescobol every weekend to stay in shape.
We played frescobol on the beach all afternoon.

senses.definition: a sport played on a small court with a low net and wooden paddles, primarily in Barbados

examples.x: Road tennis is very popular in Barbados.
They installed a new road tennis court in the neighborhood.
Road tennis combines elements of table tennis and lawn tennis.
We watched an exciting road tennis match yesterday.
He bought a new paddle for road tennis.

senses.definition: an indoor racket and ball game that combines elements of lawn tennis and real tennis

examples.x: He mastered the unique techniques required for sticke.
They enjoyed a competitive game of sticke over the weekend.
She scored a point with a powerful sticke backhand.
The sticke rackets were specially designed for the game.
He watched a professional sticke match to learn new strategies.

senses.definition: a traditional Basque racket sport played with a ball and a wooden racket

examples.x: The xare tournament drew athletes from all over the region.
The xare court is smaller than a tennis court.
He won his first xare championship last year.
The local club offers xare lessons for beginners.
